page: Amazon Web Services t4g.medium is a burstable general-purpose server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture running at 2.5 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 instruction cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage, and lacks dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks reflect its burstable, budget-oriented design. In single-core stress-ng testing, the instance achieves an average score of 1512.88, placing it in the middle tier. However, multi-core performance is weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 2867.47 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1666.0, both falling into the lower performance tiers. Memory bandwidth is also modest, measuring 35.15 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 17.23 GB/sec for large block write operations. Database and web serving capabilities are limited, with static web serving throughput at 2.23 GB/sec and Redis SET operations reaching 2.11 million operations per second.

The t4g.medium offers a highly cost-efficient profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization. Because it uses a burstable CPU allocation model, it is designed to accumulate CPU credits during idle periods to handle occasional performance spikes. This makes the instance suitable for low-traffic web servers, small database operations, microservices, and development or testing environments that do not require continuous high CPU utilization.
description: Amazon Web Services t4g.medium is a burstable, budget-friendly server instance utilizing the arm64 architecture. Powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, it features 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 4.0 GB of system memory. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate moderate single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 1512.88, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the lower tiers. With a burstable CPU allocation model, this instance provides a cost-effective solution for workloads with variable resource demands. It is best suited for light web serving, development environments, microservices, and small database operations that do not require sustained high CPU utilization.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.medium is a burstable arm64 server featuring 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and an AWS Graviton processor. It is designed for cost-effective handling of workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.medium is a burstable arm64 instance with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AWS Graviton processor.
tagline: A cost-efficient, burstable arm64 instance designed for light workloads, development environments, and applications with variable CPU utilization demands.

Features 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 4.0 GB of system memory
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for cost-efficient scaling
